name: FileBlockShredding
definition: Sysmon Event ID 28 records that Sysmon detected and blocked file shredding behavior.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event is generated when Sysmon detects and blocks file shredding from tools such as SDelete.

key_fields.explanation: The file targeted for shredding. Prioritize logs, tools, archives, and payloads in user-writable or temporary paths.
The process that attempted shredding and the join key to Event ID 1.
Hash values, when present, support triage of the file targeted for destruction.

false_positives: Approved secure-delete tools may be used by administrators under documented data-handling procedures.
Privacy tools and backup cleanup jobs can shred files during normal operations.
detection_notes: Alert when TargetFilename is under C:\Users\, C:\ProgramData\, C:\Windows\Temp, or AppData and Image resembles SDelete or an unapproved secure-delete binary. Microsoft documents Event 28 as blocked file shredding from tools such as SDelete; this supports T1070.004 File Deletion when an actor attempts to remove intrusion artifacts or evidence. Correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1 and retrieve related Event 23 archives or backups immediately.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 28
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=28 | table _time, host, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 03:07:00.000
Image: C:\Users\Public\sdelete.exe
TargetFilename: C:\Users\Public\loot.zip
Hashes: SHA256=REDACTED
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 28
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 28
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 28
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=28
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 28
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 28
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 28
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 28
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.meaning: Sysmon detected and blocked file shredding.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: Sysinternals names SDelete as an example shredding tool, not the only possible source.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret Image with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 28 mean?
faqs.answer: Sysmon blocked an attempted file-shredding operation.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 28 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Approved secure-deletion workflows can invoke shredding tools.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 28 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review Image, TargetFilename, ProcessGuid, user context, and whether the deleted material relates to an investigation.
